ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class (FOODON_03301103); no specific yuzu green tea entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A covers tea and tea-based beverages as the closest defensible parent; no specific yuzu-flavoured green tea code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for yuzu green tea beverage. No suitable parent code identified without risk of fabrication.

preparation_method: blended with yuzu citrus syrup or juice
served chilled or at room temperature

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium-sized cup of yuzu green tea as served at KOI Thé, approximately 500 ml, which is the standard medium serving size for Taiwanese bubble tea and specialty tea chains in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, green, brewed (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 14355) adapted with yuzu citrus syrup sugar contribution estimated from USDA FoodData Central branded yuzu beverage data and standard sugar syrup addition for sweetened tea drinks
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base green tea values (plain brewed green tea, per 100 ml) taken from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 14355. Yuzu citrus syrup contribution estimated: a medium sweetened yuzu green tea at a Taiwanese tea chain typically contains approximately 15–20 g added sugar per 500 ml serving (3–4 g per 100 ml), consistent with USDA branded yuzu beverage and sweetened green tea entries. Energy, carbohydrate, and sugar values adjusted accordingly. Protein and fat remain effectively zero as this is a tea-based beverage with no dairy or protein additions in the standard formulation. All zero macros are justified below in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened green tea and sugar-sweetened beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for yuzu green tea specifically. GI estimated at 45 based on: (1) plain green tea has a GI of approximately 0–1 (negligible glycemic carbohydrates); (2) the sugar syrup added to sweetened Taiwanese tea drinks raises the effective GI toward that of sucrose-sweetened beverages (GI ~60–65 for sucrose per University of Sydney GI Database); (3) the dilution effect and tea polyphenols are known to moderate postprandial glucose response, supporting a blended estimate of approximately 45 for a lightly sweetened green tea drink. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving = (45 × 20 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 9.0. This estimate is consistent with published analogous values for lightly sweetened green tea beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content; green tea polyphenols may support vascular health
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 2 mg sodium per 100 ml and no added salt, yuzu green tea poses minimal sodium concern for hypertensive individuals, though the added sugar content should be monitored if consuming multiple servings daily.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content raises glycemic load; standard sweetness level contributes approximately 20 g sugar per medium serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request reduced or zero sugar when ordering yuzu green tea, as the standard medium serving contains approximately 20 g of added sugar (glycemic load ~9), which can meaningfully raise postprandial blood glucose; unsweetened or half-sugar versions are preferable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; green tea catechins and yuzu flavonoids may offer cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Yuzu green tea contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and provides green tea catechins (EGCG) and yuzu-derived flavonoids that have been associated with modest cardioprotective effects in observational studies; however, regular consumption of the sweetened version contributes added sugar that should be factored into total daily intake for cardiovascular risk management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yuzu Green Tea (Medium) is a Taiwanese-style specialty tea beverage sold at KOI Thé, a popular Taiwanese tea chain with outlets across Singapore. The drink is made by combining brewed green tea with yuzu citrus juice or yuzu-flavoured syrup, resulting in a refreshing, lightly tart, and floral beverage. Yuzu (Citrus junos) is a Japanese and East Asian citrus fruit prized for its distinctive aromatic zest and juice, which imparts a complex flavour profile combining notes of lemon, grapefruit, and mandarin. The medium size is approximately 500 ml. The drink is typically sweetened to the customer's preference (standard, less sugar, half sugar, or no sugar) and served chilled. Nutritionally, the standard sweetened version contains approximately 16 kcal per 100 ml, with carbohydrates (primarily from added sugar syrup) as the dominant macronutrient at approximately 4 g per 100 ml. Protein and fat are naturally zero as this is a tea-based beverage with no dairy or protein additions. Green tea provides small amounts of potassium, catechins (notably EGCG), and trace minerals. Yuzu contributes a small amount of vitamin C. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low GI category) due to the moderating effect of tea polyphenols on glucose absorption, though the glycemic load per medium serving is approximately 9, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The beverage is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and halal-observant consumers. It is gluten-free and dairy-free in its standard formulation. Clinically, the primary concern is added sugar; requesting reduced or zero sugar is recommended for diabetic or weight-conscious consumers.

KOI Thé yuzu green tea nutrition
Is yuzu green tea suitable for diabetics?
How much sugar is in yuzu green tea?

Yuzu green tea GI glycemic index

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for KOI Thé yuzu green tea exists in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy plain brewed green tea (entry 14355) with sugar contribution estimated from standard Taiwanese tea chain sweetening practices and USDA branded yuzu beverage data. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for sweetened green tea and sucrose. Confidence is medium rather than low because the adaptation methodology is transparent, the base source is approved, and the estimated values are consistent with the known composition of this beverage category.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero: this is a tea-based beverage with no protein-containing ingredients (no dairy, no soy, no protein additions). Confirmed consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 14355 for plain brewed green tea. 2. fat_g is naturally zero: no fat-containing ingredients are present in the standard yuzu green tea formulation. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all naturally zero by product formulation. 3. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero: tea infusions and citrus syrups do not contribute measurable dietary fibre in liquid form. 4. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero: no animal-derived ingredients present. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy green tea entry and is not a meaningful contributor in this beverage. 6. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source for this beverage category. 7. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source for this beverage category. 8. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source for this beverage category. 9. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source for this beverage category. 10. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported; no animal-derived ingredients present. 11. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source for this beverage category. 12. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported; naturally absent in tea beverages. 13. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source for this beverage category. 14. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source for this beverage category. 15. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source for this beverage category. 16. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source for this beverage category. 17. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source for this beverage category. 18. glycemic_index set to 45 (low GI category) as an analogous estimate; see gi_source for full methodology. glycemic_load_per_serving calculated as (45 × 20 g carbohydrate per 500 ml serving) / 100 = 9.0. 19. This record covers the standard sweetened medium serving. Nutritional values will vary with sugar level customisation (no sugar, less sugar, half sugar, standard). 20. The food_name 'Yuzu Green Tea (Medium)' is used as the canonical English name; the input food_name 'M-Yuzu Green Tea' and description '中-金柚绿茶' are preserved as aliases. 21. Cuisine is classified as Taiwanese reflecting the brand origin (KOI Thé is a Taiwanese tea chain); multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules as this is a Singapore-served item from a Taiwanese chain, requiring English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi coverage.
